name: The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope - Circular from Calasparra
name_original: El Santuario Virgen de Nuestra Señora de la Esperanza — Circular desde Calasparra
region_intro: Hiking around Calasparra offers diverse landscapes shaped by its mountainous terrain and the confluence of four rivers, including the Segura and Argos. The region features lush green riverine areas, such as the Almadenes Canyon, which contrast with more arid surroundings. Hikers can explore varied topography, from river valleys and canyons to the unique Calasparra rice fields. This natural setting provides a range of trails for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Calasparra The most popular hiking route is The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope - Circular from Calasparra, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Sanctuary, which is set within a natural cave by the Segura River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sanctuary of Hope loop from Cañada de Manrique, an easy 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This trail offers a shorter loop through the natural surroundings of the Sanctuary. Local hikers also love the Sanctuary of Hope – Entrance to the Forest loop from Bosque de Cañaverosa, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through riverine forest and natural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Calasparra is defined by the Segura River, the dramatic Almadenes Canyon, and the distinctive Calasparra rice fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate paths. The routes in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 190 hikers have used komoot to explore Calasparra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Calasparra?
A: Calasparra offers over 20 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix, with about 10 easy routes, 13 moderate options, and one more challenging trail, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Calasparra?
A: Yes, Calasparra has several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33734280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Hope loop from Cañada de Manrique&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile (5.7 km) path that offers a pleasant stroll through the natural surroundings of the Sanctuary, perfect for a family outing.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, shaped by the Segura and Argos rivers. You can explore spectacular features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, the lush riverine forest of the Almadenes Canyon, and the confluence of the Moratalla and Segura rivers. The famous Calasparra rice fields also present a unique and picturesque landscape.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Calasparra?
A: Yes, many routes in Calasparra are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e894921197/the-sanctuary-of-our-lady-of-hope-circular-from-calasparra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope - Circular from Calasparra&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7-mile (11.2 km) trail that explores the area around the Sanctuary.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can be visited while hiking?
A: Calasparra blends natural exploration with rich history. The revered &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt; is uniquely situated within a natural cave. You can also find ancient rock paintings in caves like Abrigos del Pozo and Cueva de Los Monigotes, some recognized as UNESCO World Heritage sites, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Calasparra?
A: The trails in Calasparra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush river valleys to unique rice fields, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Calasparra?
A: While Calasparra itself is accessible by public transport, direct public transport links to specific trailheads outside the town center may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to trail starting points, especially for routes further afield.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Calasparra?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Calasparra are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking trails in Calasparra?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain and river canyons offer several scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cañón de Almadenes&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning panoramic views of the dramatic Almadenes Canyon and the Segura River carving through the landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Calasparra?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and attractions such as the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Hope. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the komoot tour details for information on designated parking areas close to the starting points.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through Calasparra?
A: Yes, Calasparra is part of larger hiking networks. Major long-distance routes like the GR 7 and the GR 127 (Segura River) traverse the region, offering opportunities for extensive exploration of the diverse landscapes for those seeking multi-day adventures.

Q: What makes the Almadenes Canyon a significant hiking spot?
A: The Almadenes Canyon is a protected natural area renowned for its 'incredible gorge over the river Segura.' It features a rich riverine forest, dramatic rocky terrain, and ancient rock paintings. It's a prime location for both hiking and water-based activities like rafting, offering a unique blend of adventure and natural beauty.
tips: The Sanctuary is located in a place 6 km by road from the town. The hermitage houses two images of the Virgen de la Esperanza, patron saint of Calasparra, who are worshiped together. The primitive hermitage (XVII) was in one of the caves on the Segura river, a natural shelter for shepherds, to which new rooms and buildings have been added with the expansion of the cult. The creation of terraces up to the river has been succeeded in modern times by the creation of service areas: parking lots, restaurant, hotel, etc.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope
